claim: The deaths-back-projection that produces the snapshot's total-case band reproduces Imperial Method 2, with the onset-to-death growth correction taken from the Rosello 2015 BDBV gamma and the CFR set from the US CDC prior-outbreak aggregate. The implementation is original code.
value: total_cases = deaths * (1 + r/beta)^alpha / CFR; r = ln(2)/tau; Rosello gamma alpha 4.42, beta 0.388 per day; growth correction 1.7003 at tau=14; May-20 band 612 to 942 at deaths 144, CFR 40 to 26
source_refs: Rosello A, et al. eLife 2015. DOI 10.7554/eLife.09015, Table 5, Isiro 2012 outbreak fit.; Imperial College MRC GIDA and WHO. Estimation of the size of the BDBV outbreak in DRC: 20 May 2026 update.; US CDC. History of Ebola Outbreaks.; lovs/lovs_death_back_projection.py total_cases_from_deaths and sensitivity_grid.
public_action: Keep total_cases_from_deaths linked to this audit row. Treat the 612 to 942 band as a reproduction on the snapshot death count, not as the Imperial reference. If the Rosello gamma or the doubling-time central changes, update this audit row and NUMBERS_AUDIT together.
public_note: Detailed audit IDs and review locators are withheld from this public export.

claim: BDBV calibration commitments are block-scoped: a later snapshot can carry older blocks and append new blocks, while each block preserves its own pin date, original horizon, resolution timestamp, elapsed-days clock, and remaining-days clock.
value: per block: horizon_days = date(resolves_at) - date(pinned_at); remaining_days = date(resolves_at) - date(snapshot_as_of)
source_refs: data/calibration-ledger.json calibration-block:bdbv-uga-cod-2026:2026-05-20.; refresh_pipeline.py calibration_clock.
